_comment: Example: Bee entity with full relational scope blocks. Use this as a template when adding scoped evidence to any entity.
name: Bee
description: Eusocial pollinator demonstrating hexagonal optimization, waggle-dance communication, and distributed decision-making. Each attribute below shows a fully-filled relational scope block.

scope.definition: Ratio of wax used per unit of honey stored in hexagonal comb versus circular cells at same volume. Hexagonal packing minimizes perimeter for equal area — the honeycomb conjecture (proved 1999).

evidence.source: Hales (1999) mathematical proof of honeycomb conjecture; confirmed by engineering measurement of comb mass vs. storage capacity.

condition.environment: ambient temperature 32-35°C (maintained by worker bees inside hive)
constraints: wax must be at working temperature
cells must be constructed by experienced workers
horizontal orientation required for cell construction

scope.falsifiability: If bees can be shown to construct non-hexagonal cells that store equal honey with equal wax, the efficiency claim fails. No such observation exists.
scope.measurement_limits: Efficiency ratio is for honey storage. Cell shape for brood rearing varies slightly.

scope.definition: Angular accuracy of flower direction encoded in waggle dance (relative to sun azimuth), confirmed by observing scout bees and tracking recruits to destination.

evidence.source: von Frisch (1967) Nobel Prize research; replicated by Dyer (2002), Seeley (2010).

condition.environment: sunny conditions (sun visible as azimuth reference)
constraints: scout must have visited flower within 2 hours
dance must be performed on vertical comb surface

scope.falsifiability: In overcast conditions without UV penetration, dance accuracy drops significantly. Direction accuracy fails in zero-gravity (no gravity reference for dance orientation).
scope.measurement_limits: Accuracy degrades for sources > 12km. Dance duration (distance encoding) is approximate ± 15%.

scope.definition: Fraction of scout bees that must visit and report on a new nest site before swarm departure is initiated. Quorum sensing prevents premature commitment.

evidence.source: Seeley & Visscher (2004); Seeley (2010) 'Honeybee Democracy'; multi-year observation at Cornell.
cross_domain_examples: ant colony quorum sensing for nest relocation
neuron firing threshold in cortical columns
democratic tipping point models (Centola et al.)
T-cell activation threshold in immune response

condition.environment: swarming season, cluster hanging from tree branch
constraints: competing site reports must converge through dance attrition
piping signal must initiate from winning site advocates

scope.falsifiability: If a swarm can be induced to depart with fewer than 5% of scouts committed, quorum sensing fails as a model. No such induction has succeeded.
scope.measurement_limits: Threshold is approximate — varies ±5% by colony size and season.

links.note: Flight paths show logarithmic spiral search patterns.
Parallel quorum sensing architecture at 0.15 threshold.
Both distribute intelligence without central nodes.
